firmware.provisioning: device was already flashed by the maintainer; this run did not build or change firmware

acceptance.authorization: explicit maintainer authorization for the named blink device
acceptance.observation: MGMT state transitions and sanitized ESPHome Native API logs; no camera or serial session
acceptance.bounded_runner: timeout with SIGINT followed by MGMT graceful shutdown
acceptance.runner_exit: expected timeout status 124 after MGMT completed graceful SIGINT shutdown
results: make build passed on the exact feat/esphome revision
built-in multicast DNS resolved the unchanged .local target
Noise handshake and ESPHome hello completed
entity discovery found the expected switch and binary sensor
initial and pushed binary-sensor states reached MGMT
at least eight on-to-off convergence cycles issued real switch commands
device logs reached the MGMT endpoint logger
MGMT handled the bounded interrupt and shut down cleanly

limitations: The physical LED output was not independently observed by a camera or second observer; device state and firmware logs confirm the command path.
No firmware was built or flashed during this acceptance run.
This proves the blink switch and binary-sensor slice, not Fan, Light, Number, Button, conveyor safety, reconnect, or factory-scale behavior.
The checked-in demonstration key is intentionally public and is not suitable for an untrusted network.
